Best for workloads with many small kernels and fixed\nlaunch parameters; structures with variable shapes or per-step\ncontrol flow cannot be captured directly.       "description": "Set torch.backends.cudnn.benchmark=True so that when a cuDNN\nconvolution is called with a new set of size parameters, cuDNN runs\nmultiple algorithms, benchmarks them to find the fastest one, and\nreuses it for the rest of the process for the same size parameters.\nDisabling it (benchmark=False) makes cuDNN deterministically select\nan algorithm, possibly at the cost of reduced performance. Best for workloads with many small kernels and fixed\nlaunch parameters; structures with variable shapes or per-step\ncontrol flow cannot be captured directly. See the CUDA C++\nProgramming Guide CUDA Graphs section for the full set of capture\nrestrictions and the API surface.",
